pullRequest.title: DOM-54752 Dom 54752 UI add new endpoint modal

nodes.body: We can do it later: A way for achieving this should be by wrapping the form methods with a function that calls the spy (with the full args list) and then calling the regular implementation
Is the modal visibility state necessary at this scope? I think it can live at the view scope
I think the state should be in the container component. The view component should be just the dummy part of the component
yeah it is not a big deal, I try to always have the state as close as possible to the component that concerns about it to avoid unnecessary prop drilling. Not necessary to change it though.
It is usually a nice to have for the variable to be self descriptive, instead of setting px a as comment you can name de var: `ADD_MODAL_WIDTH_PX`
Agree
nit, have you tried `as const`?

Nop, but I improved this by moving the array outside the component and defining a constant as `RadioSpec<T>`. The value then were automatically typed with the possible values of EndpointAccessPermissions
why isn't prevProvider assigned with `const { provider } = formInstance.getFieldsValue();` provider's value as initial value?
Done

If I am not missing anything, this form instance is not reactive. This can lead to issues with the `prevProvider` logic. Have you checked https://ant.design/components/form#formusewatch for watching form values?
Replaced with useWatch

The prev + useEffect pattern is discouraged by the react team: https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#adjusting-some-state-when-a-prop-changes. I don't like the new pattern too much so the change is up to you.

Regardless of the comment above, prevProvider value should not care about `model` value. We should always keep them in sync.
I'll go with the React's piece of advice
Have you given a try to https://ant.design/components/form#formusewatch? I think that with new hook this pattern can be deprecated. Specially here that it is repeated a couple of times
Using `useWatch`
have you checked that this doesn't affect other modals?

Modal previous implementations override this style by setting all paddings to 0 (btw the prop they used is deprecated). This property should help the rest of the implementations and we can remove the deprecated property
There is a PR that removes this from some days ago: https://github.com/cerebrotech/domino/pull/40136/files, is it correct to readd it?
Good catch. Actually this should not be added. It was removed
I don't know if any of the logic gets broken but you should always set the prev value regardless of the model?.length value

The idea was to fulfill the textarea when the model was selected. Anyway, I've been thinking about removing this condition, as the configuration schema does not depend on the model but only for the chosen provider
